name: ectd

mif.reason: eCTD (FDA electronic Common Technical Document) is an electronic submission packaging/transport format orthogonal to MIF Level-3 markdown structure. It packages a clinical-submission artifact into the regulatory module/backbone structure (M1-M5 + the XML backbone), built directly from the SOURCES — the findings corpus and its primary-source citations — never from a rendered report. The citation-leak gate still applies; the canonical MIF Level-3 source of truth lives in the generic report channel.

description: Package a clinical-submission artifact into the FDA eCTD module structure (M1 regional admin, M2 summaries, M3 quality, M4 nonclinical, M5 clinical study reports) plus the XML backbone, built directly from the SOURCES — a topic's surviving findings and their primary-source citations. Never built from a rendered report. Verify-live eCTD v4.0.
